steps.title: Find the passage's dual focus
steps.content: The passage spends its opening establishing what the Federalist Papers are (collection, authors, publication, purpose), then pivots with Though to make authorship—both its certainty and uncertainty—an explicit focus.

steps.title: Apply the focus to the answers
steps.content:
B directly captures this: it describes the collection (85 essays by Hamilton, Jay, and Madison, published 1787-88, arguing for Constitutional ratification) and addresses authorship (most essays have certain attribution, but some like No. 52 remain uncertain—possibly Hamilton or Madison).

The other choices either reduce the purpose to a single element (arguments or publication history) or misrepresent the authorship as multiple-author writing rather than uncertain single authorship.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
